title: Bob Kaneko Oral History
creator: Tessaku
description: See this link for the most complete and recent information on this record: https://repository.californiarevealed.org/node/454973 .

Oral history interview with Bob Kaneko, who was a child at the time of the Japanese American incarceration during WWII. Bob was living in Berkeley, California and was sent to the Tule Lake concentration camp in California with his family who then returned to Berekley after the war. Nearly 120,000 Japanese Americans were uprooted from their West Coast homes with the signing of Executive Order 9066 in February of 1942 and sent to inland concentration camps for four to five years.
